summary: This page concludes an example of time inversion by showing how a signal f(t) becomes f(-t), both graphically and in piecewise mathematical form. It then introduces combined time operations of the form f(at-b), explaining equivalent sequences of shifting and scaling, and begins the next section on useful signal models such as the step, impulse, and exponential functions.

figures.caption: Fig. 1.13 An example of time inversion, showing a signal f(t) in plot (a) and its time-inverted version f(-t) in plot (b).
